channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Cursor Launches a New AI Agent Experience to Take on Claude Code and Codex
item.description: As Cursor launches the next generation of its product, the AI coding startup has to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic more directly than ever.

item.title: I Migrated to a Monorepo in 2 Days. Things Broke.
item.description: I migrated my side project, Inner Anchor, from two separate repos to a monorepo. It took two days, things broke in production, and my users saw a white screen. But I'd do it again because I don't...

item.title: I’m sick of $200 SaaS boilerplates that leak tenant data. Here is how to build real isolation.
item.description: I’m sick of "SaaS boilerplates" that charge you $200 just to wrap a framework and leave you to figure out multi-tenant database isolation yourself when your first enterprise customer signs up....

item.title: Proof of Humanity™
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV April Fools Challenge What I Built To prove you're human, you must assemble Flätpack furniture. One step is irrelevant. Robots cannot detect irony. Demo...

item.title: I Built an AI Agent Harness in Go
item.description: Hello there! I've been using AI tools a lot lately. ChatGPT, Claude, local models with Ollama. They're great for answering questions, but I wanted something that could actually do things. Search...

item.title: I built a browser extension that shows you everything websites store on your device and the results are kind of wild
item.description: I want to start with a question. When was the last time you actually looked at what a website has stored on your device? Not just cookies. Everything. IndexedDB. Local storage. Cache storage....

item.title: TrueNAS Setup Guide: Enterprise Security for Your Homelab
item.description: Last month I rebuilt my TrueNAS server from scratch after a drive failure. What started as a simple disk replacement turned into a full security audit — and I realized my homelab storage had been...

item.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
item.description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

item.title: The Quality Crisis in AI-Generated Everything: Building Systems That Earn Trust
item.description: The Quality Crisis in AI-Generated Everything: Building Systems That Earn Trust Here's what the data actually shows: 84% of developers use AI tools 45% of AI-generated code contains security...

item.title: Building a Document Classification and Extraction Pipeline with Gemini Vision API
item.description: Introduction Businesses receive hundreds of documents daily — invoices, receipts, contracts — arriving as PDFs, images, and spreadsheets, all mixed together. Someone has to open each one, figure...

item.title: Stop Chasing Scroll with JavaScript: A Deep Dive into CSS Scroll-Driven Animations
item.description: I published a deep-dive guide about CSS scroll-driven animations and how they change the architectural layer where motion is controlled. This is not just about making parallax “look cooler”. It is...

item.title: The Exit Criteria Pattern: Know When to Stop Iterating with AI
item.description: Here's a pattern I see constantly: a developer asks AI to refactor a function, gets a decent result, then spends 45 minutes on follow-up prompts trying to make it "perfect." The final version is...

item.title: Hisense’s art-inspired CanvasTV has hit a new low price
item.description: Samsung’s releasing a new version of The Frame sometime this year, though the exact date is still unknown and, based on past models, it’s likely to be pricey. If you’d rather not wait or want to...

item.title: Flipboard just launched Surf, its new social app and feed reader
item.description: Surf is a slightly hard app to explain. It's sort of three things: a client for fediverse apps like Bluesky and Mastodon; a feed reader that lets you subscribe to almost any website, podcast, or...

item.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
item.description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

item.title: Anthropic Says That Claude Contains Its Own Kind of Emotions
item.description: Researchers at the company found representations inside of Claude that perform functions similar to human feelings.

item.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
item.description: Google Vids logo surrounded by various video editing UI

item.title: New York lawmakers want 3D-printer companies to block the creation of ‘ghost guns’
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul and other New York state lawmakers want 3D-printer companies to block the printing of components used to create "ghost guns" - firearms without serial numbers that can be...

item.title: Razer gaming accessories include a $25 Taco Bell gift card at Best Buy
item.description: Razer is summoning hungry gamers to Best Buy, where shoppers can get a free $25 Taco Bell gift card with the purchase of a gaming accessory today only. A whopping 41 items are eligible, ranging...

item.title: It’s not easy to get depression-detecting AI through the FDA
item.description: For the past seven years, the California-based startup Kintsugi has been developing AI designed to detect signs of depression and anxiety from a person's speech. But after failing to secure FDA...

item.title: Despite EV headwinds, Rivian’s sales are up
item.description: It's rough out there for the pure EV companies, but despite all the challenges, Rivian says its on target for a historic year. Ahead of the launch of the company's more affordable R2 electric SUV,...

item.title: YouTube’s TV takeover continues with 24/7 streaming ‘Stations’
item.description: When Coachella returns to YouTube next week, the music festival will be offering more than just live performances. Viewers will also be able to tune into something YouTube calls Stations - 24/7...

item.title: Cisco Patches 9.8 CVSS IMC and SSM Flaws Allowing Remote System Compromise
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to...

item.title: Samsung announces 2026 Frame TVs and the Pro costs less than last year’s
item.description: Samsung has announced some more details about The Frame and Frame Pro, and the most interesting news is that this year's Frame Pro will be a little less expensive across sizes. While last year's...

item.title: The boring, insidious world of the womanosphere
item.description: Usha Vance has a new podcast: Storytime with the Second Lady. It's exactly what it sounds like. Each episode begins with a brief introduction, after which JD Vance's wife reads a children's story....

item.title: Tesla’s sales recover slightly, but the trend lines are all bad
item.description: Tesla's first quarter sales report is out, and depending on how you look at it, the numbers are good but also bad. The company's sales are up a modest 6 percent year over year, but that's probably...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy...

item.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next. As the war...

item.title: Researchers Uncover Mining Operation Using ISO Lures to Spread RATs and Crypto Miners
item.description: A financially motivated operation codenamed REF1695 has been observed leveraging fake installers to deploy remote access trojans (RATs) and cryptocurrency miners since November 2023. "Beyond...

item.title: The State of Trusted Open Source Report
item.description: In December 2025, we shared the first-ever The State of Trusted Open Source report, featuring insights from our product data and customer base on open source consumption across our catalog of...
